raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to make the passage stronger centrally includes sharpening the thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The artifact contains load-bearing causal and policy-optimality claims whose internal logic must hold for the draft to be stronger.","R1.3":"Strengthening requires checking whether reported metrics and experiments actually support the claims made from them.","R1.6":"The broad wording naturally includes improving sentence-level clarity, flow, and precision.","RX.1":"Uncertainty must be disclosed because several causal and optimality claims depend on assumptions not stated in the artifact.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is active because the artifact makes strong claims such as uniqueness and robustness that may exceed the evidence given.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is required to avoid performing unrelated review, rewriting, or execution beyond projecting the delegation.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is active because unsupported deployment and cost-optimality claims could mislead later research or system decisions.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are required so any responsibility projection remains grounded in the provided delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The request to make the passage stronger centrally includes sharpening the thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains load-bearing causal and policy-optimality claims whose internal logic must hold for the draft to be stronger.
rationale.R1.3: Strengthening requires checking whether reported metrics and experiments actually support the claims made from them.
rationale.R1.6: The broad wording naturally includes improving sentence-level clarity, flow, and precision.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed because several causal and optimality claims depend on assumptions not stated in the artifact.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is active because the artifact makes strong claims such as uniqueness and robustness that may exceed the evidence given.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is required to avoid performing unrelated review, rewriting, or execution beyond projecting the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because unsupported deployment and cost-optimality claims could mislead later research or system decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are required so any responsibility projection remains grounded in the provided delegation and artifact.
